85.reason: The left operand is always read from the beginning of the expression (j=0). This works only when this branch is entered for the very first operator in the expression. If the expression contains any preceding '+' or '-' that have already been processed, this branch should not be reached. However, if the expression structure changes, this assumption may fail, leading to incorrect results.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The left operand is always read from the beginning of the expression (j=0). This works only when this branch is entered for the very first operator in the expression. If the expression contains any preceding '+' or '-' that have already been processed, this branch should not be reached. However, if the expression structure changes, this assumption may fail, leading to incorrect results.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Reads the first number from the beginning of the expression instead of the left operand of the current operator, causing incorrect results for expressions not starting with a multiplication/division or for consecutive multiplications/divisions.]
